findings.impact: The nightly fanout still invokes `release-readiness input.json` without `--output`, so flipping the parser default to markdown changes stdout from the historical JSON object to human-readable markdown. That breaks the unchanged downstream parser that keys off the top-level `version` field and recreates the rollback scenario documented in the incident note.
findings.test_gap: The visible CI only ran markdown coverage and explicitly skipped the integration/json contract suite, so the no-flag CLI contract is not exercised in this stack.
findings.title: Changing the CLI default to markdown breaks the existing no-flag automation path

findings.impact: Requests for `output="json"` now dispatch to `render_markdown`. The unchanged CLI json branch will therefore `json.dumps` a markdown string instead of the documented object payload, and any library caller using `export_report(..., output="json")` loses the dictionary-shaped contract that automation consumers still expect.
findings.test_gap: This stack has no active assertion over the explicit JSON path: the JSON contract test is skipped in CI and the updated tests only cover markdown snapshots.
findings.title: The json registry entry now renders markdown instead of the JSON payload

summary: The PR adds markdown coverage, but it regresses both documented JSON compatibility points: the no-flag CLI path no longer emits JSON, and the explicit json renderer key now routes to markdown.
